sources.title: WCAG 2.2 Understanding: Target Size (Minimum)
borrowed: Freeze minimum pointer target work at 44px.
Treat grouped pills and small route toggles as deliberate target-size work, not incidental styling.
rejected: Did not enlarge the entire shell density or replace existing pills with oversized blocks.

sources.title: WCAG 2.2 Understanding: Reflow
borrowed: Proved 390px and 320px reflow without horizontal overflow.
Kept focus map and target-size wrappers from breaking narrow grid layouts.
rejected: Did not collapse the route family into an unrelated mobile-only information architecture.

sources.title: WCAG 2.2 Understanding: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
borrowed: Drawer and mission-stack focus return is verified around sticky regions.
Support-region scrolling switches to auto in reduced-motion mode.
rejected: Did not add floating helper chrome that could itself obscure focus targets.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA APG: Dialog (Modal) Pattern
borrowed: Initial focus moves into the dialog surface.
Escape and focus return are explicit shared rules for both pharmacy drawers.
rejected: Did not force every drawer close button to be the only initial focus target.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA APG: Keyboard Interface
borrowed: Kept shell keyboard order consistent and predictable.
Avoided introducing composite-widget arrow-key logic where plain buttons were sufficient.
rejected: Did not convert same-shell route toggles into a heavier tab system.

sources.title: Playwright Accessibility Testing
borrowed: Use Playwright for route-level accessibility assertions.
Keep accessibility proof close to real browser behavior instead of static markup checks only.
rejected: Did not rely on generated axe-only output as the whole proof surface.

sources.title: Playwright ARIA Snapshots
borrowed: Publish dedicated aria snapshot artifacts for shell and drawer surfaces.
Use snapshot capture to freeze semantic drift in the route family.
rejected: Did not make aria snapshots the only acceptance gate; keyboard and focus proofs remain separate.

sources.title: Playwright Emulation
borrowed: Use reduced-motion emulation in the 365 battery.
Verify narrow mobile viewports inside the same scripts.
rejected: Did not add device-specific UI forks.

sources.title: Playwright Best Practices
borrowed: Assert stable user-facing behavior through roles, test ids, and flows.
Keep the new 365 scripts focused on distinct risks rather than one giant scenario.
rejected: Did not overfit to internal DOM depth or animation timings.

sources.title: NHS Design System: Notification Banner
borrowed: Use calm, concise state messaging for changes that need attention.
Keep warning copy separate from confirmation copy.
rejected: Did not convert the shells into full notification-banner layouts.

sources.title: NHS Design System: Error Summary
borrowed: Preserve direct access to the affected control when a state requires repair or acknowledgement.
Keep remediation cues adjacent to the relevant form control.
rejected: Did not introduce top-of-page error summaries for non-form warnings.

sources.title: NHS Accessibility Guidance
borrowed: Keep contrast, focus, and zoom behavior explicit in the audit notes.
Prefer plain language status copy over transport jargon.
rejected: Did not broaden this task into a whole-site content rewrite.

sources.title: Carbon Design System: Data Table Accessibility
borrowed: Keep dense operations tables keyboard-reachable without turning every row into a custom widget.
Preserve clear header-to-cell meaning in the pharmacy truth matrix.
rejected: Did not import Carbon table styling or interaction patterns wholesale.
